3
Chat with Instance
✗ FAIL
What I did

Clicked "Chat (⌘T)" button. Chat view opened with instance selector pre-set to "My OpenClaw". Typed "wake up" into the message textbox (ref e6). Pressed Enter. Waited 10 seconds.

Exact error from UI
wake up  01:26 AM
Error: Gateway returned 404: Not Found  01:26 AM
Root cause (for Brook)

MultiClaw sends chat messages to http://127.0.0.1:40000/v1/chat/completions. That endpoint does not exist on the OpenClaw gateway — it returns HTTP 404. This may also involve a missing or mismatched auth token. Brook to investigate correct gateway chat API.

Verdict
FAIL
Chat returns 404 every time. Core feature non-functional. See BUG-002.

✅ Color picker with 6 options (Blue, Green, Orange, Red, Purple, Teal)

Verdict
PASS
Instance creation works end-to-end. UX is clean.
5
Delete Instance
⚠ CONDITIONAL PASS
What I did

Right-clicked "Cedar Test Instance" row in sidebar. Context menu appeared with "Start" and "Delete…" options. Clicked "Delete…". Confirmation dialog appeared in the sidebar. Clicked the red "Delete" button.

Bug encountered

After clicking "Delete" in the renderer dialog, the app appeared to freeze. The dialog remained visible and the instance was not removed from the sidebar for an extended period.

Root cause: The main process fires a second native OS confirmation dialog (dialog.showMessageBox) when the IPC instances:delete is called. This native dialog is modal and blocks renderer interaction, but is invisible to agent-browser (which connects only to the renderer). The instance IS eventually deleted once the native dialog is handled.

# main.ts line 75-87
const { response, checkboxChecked } = await dialog.showMessageBox(win, {
  type: 'warning',
  title: 'Delete Instance',
  message: `Delete "${inst.name}"?`,  // ← second confirmation
  buttons: ['Cancel', 'Delete'],
})
IPC verification after delete
window.multiclaw.instances.list() →
[{"id":"my-agent","name":"My OpenClaw","port":40000,"status":"running"}]
// Cedar Test Instance is gone ✓
Verdict
CONDITIONAL PASS
Delete works but has a double-confirmation UX bug. See BUG-001.
6
Open in Browser
⚠ CONDITIONAL PASS
What I did

With "My OpenClaw" running, searched the instance view for an "Open in Browser" button or link. Found none. Navigated directly to http://localhost:40000.

Missing UI element

The instance toolbar contains only "■ Stop" and "↺ Restart". No browser-open button exists. The shell.openExternal IPC handler is defined in main.ts and exposed via preload, but is never called from the renderer — the feature is wired but has no UI entrypoint. See BUG-003.

Gateway web UI status

The gateway web UI at http://localhost:40000 loads and renders correctly. However it shows:

Health: Offline
"unauthorized: gateway token missing (open the dashboard URL and paste the token in Control UI settings)"

The gateway itself is running; the web UI requires an auth token to connect. This is expected behavior for a fresh browser session.

Verdict
CONDITIONAL PASS
Gateway web UI is reachable and loads. "Open in Browser" button is missing from the app. See BUG-003.

🐛 Bugs Found

BUG-001
Double confirmation dialog on instance delete
MEDIUM

Flow: 5 — Delete Instance

Description

When a user clicks "Delete…" from the right-click context menu, the renderer shows an inline confirmation dialog (Sidebar.tsx). When the user clicks "Delete" in that dialog, the main process fires a second native OS modal dialog via dialog.showMessageBox. This creates two confirmation prompts for the same action and causes the renderer to appear frozen while the native dialog is open.

Steps to reproduce
1. Right-click any instance in sidebar
2. Click "Delete…"
3. Renderer confirmation dialog appears — click "Delete"
4. App appears to freeze (native dialog now blocking)
5. Eventually: instance is deleted
Location
src/main/main.ts lines 75–87 (dialog.showMessageBox call inside instances:delete handler)
src/renderer/components/Sidebar.tsx lines 231–299 (renderer-level confirmation dialog)
Fix suggestion

Remove one of the two dialogs. The renderer dialog is already sufficient — remove the dialog.showMessageBox from the main process handler, or remove the renderer-side confirmation and rely solely on the native OS dialog.

BUG-002
Chat returns "Gateway returned 404: Not Found"
HIGH

Flow: 3 — Chat with Instance

Description

Sending any message via the Chat tab returns an error. The app POSTs to /v1/chat/completions on the gateway — this endpoint does not exist. May also involve auth token issues.

Exact error shown in UI
wake up  01:26 AM
Error: Gateway returned 404: Not Found  01:26 AM
Steps to reproduce
1. Start "My OpenClaw" instance (Flow 2 passes — instance is running)
2. Click "Chat (⌘T)"
3. Type any message in the textbox
4. Press Enter
5. Error appears immediately: "Gateway returned 404: Not Found"
Location
src/instances/manager.ts line 251:
  fetch(`http://127.0.0.1:${inst.port}/v1/chat/completions`, ...)
  // This endpoint does not exist on the OpenClaw gateway
Note

Assigned to Brook to investigate the correct gateway chat API endpoint and/or auth token flow.

BUG-003
"Open in Browser" button missing from instance toolbar
LOW

Flow: 6 — Open in Browser

Description

The instance view toolbar (visible when an instance is selected) shows only "Stop" and "Restart". There is no button to open the gateway web UI in the system browser. The IPC infrastructure for this exists (shell:openExternal handler in main.ts, exposed in preload), but no renderer component calls it.

Steps to reproduce
1. Start "My OpenClaw" (status: Running)
2. Inspect the instance toolbar — only Stop and Restart visible
3. No "Open in Browser" or external link button present
Location
src/main/main.ts lines 115–121: shell:openExternal handler exists but unused from UI
src/renderer/ — no component calls window.multiclaw.shell?.openExternal()
Fix suggestion

Add a globe/external-link icon button to the instance toolbar. On click: window.multiclaw.shell.openExternal(`http://localhost:${port}`). Only show when instance status is "running".

Overall Verdict

App health: Partial Pass. The foundational shell works — launch, start/stop, sidebar navigation, and instance creation/deletion all function correctly. The app feels solid for the basic lifecycle flows.

Critical blocker: Chat is completely broken (BUG-002). This is the core value proposition of the app — sending a message to a running agent — and it fails 100% of the time with a 404. Nothing ships until this is fixed.

Non-blocking before ship: BUG-001 (double dialog) is jarring UX — easy fix. BUG-003 (missing Open in Browser button) is a convenience feature — low effort, high value.

Ready for re-test once BUG-002 is resolved.
